extraction.one_line_summary: KEMNA Leonard won from 14-rider breakaway after catching LOPEZ Juan Pedro inside final 3km who took maglia rosa 39 seconds up
tactical_patterns: OLDANI Stefan attacked at climb base to bridge 12-minute gap for CAVENDISH's defense, teammate sacrificed stage for jersey protection.
BAHRAIN and INEOS controlled 12-minute gap collaboratively despite competing GC interests, revealing alliance to prevent Lopez from gaining excessive time.
LOPEZ Juan Pedro and KEMNA appeared to negotiate stage-versus-jersey split in final 3km rather than contesting both prizes maximally.
BORA placed three riders in GC group plus stage winner KEMNA ahead, demonstrating superior depth compared to singular team leadership models.

rider_signals.LOPEZ Juan Pedro: positive: Giro debutant gained 2.5 minutes on GC favorites on first mountain test, suggesting climbing form superior to pre-race expectations
rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: negative: First major GC name dropped with 15km remaining on moderate gradient, signaling catastrophic form collapse from 2017 winner
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: negative: Distanced with 5km to summit on home island Sicily where motivation should peak, career decline confirmed
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: neutral: Team committed OLDANI and MACHIAVELLI resources to defend pink despite only three Hungarian stages completed, revealing aggressive early-race strategy
rider_signals.LOPEZ Miguel Angel: negative: Abandoned with hip injury on Stage 4 Sicily for second time in three Giros, revealing chronic vulnerability to specific race conditions
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: negative: Lost time to main GC group on first mountain test while YATES survived despite knee injury, reversing intra-team hierarchy expectations
